description: Reusable skill to navigate to a PDF URL, go to a specified page number, and extract the text content from that page.

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template: Navigate to the given PDF URL using a browser. Wait for the PDF to fully load. Then navigate to the specified page number (e.g., by simulating clicks on page controls or using the browser's PDF viewer API). Finally, extract all visible text from the current page view and return it as a string. If any step fails, return an error message describing the issue.

url.description: Full URL of the PDF document

text.description: Extracted text from the specified page

postconditions: The text content of the specified page has been extracted and returned
Any error during loading or extraction is reported
preconditions: The PDF URL is accessible without authentication
The PDF is not corrupted and can be rendered in a browser
The browser PDF viewer supports the page navigation method used

name: extract_pdf_page_content_trajectory_browser_use_12_extract_pdf_content

challenges.expected_evidence: The extracted text matches the content of the specified page of the PDF.
challenges.failure_signal: Empty string returned, or text from wrong page, or navigation fails.
rubric: Successfully navigates to the PDF URL
Correctly identifies and navigates to the specified page
Extracts text content from that page
challenges.task: Given a PDF URL and a target page number (e.g., 5), navigate to that page and extract all visible text.

challenges.expected_evidence: The first page text is extracted correctly.
challenges.failure_signal: No output, or only partial text, or error due to missing page controls.
rubric: Navigates to PDF and waits for initial render
Handles the default display of page 1 without explicit navigation
Extracts all text from the first page
challenges.task: Given a PDF URL and page number 1, extract the content of the first page (which may not have page controls).

challenges.expected_evidence: An error message explaining the failure.
challenges.failure_signal: Script hangs, crashes, or returns empty without explanation.
rubric: Detects the failure to load the PDF (e.g., HTTP error, timeout)
Returns an informative error message rather than crashing or hanging
Does not attempt to navigate pages on a non-loaded PDF
challenges.task: Attempt to extract text from a PDF that is inaccessible (e.g., broken URL or requires authentication).

apis_tools: Browser page operations (e.g., wait_for_selector, evaluate for PDF controls)
Text extraction from DOM or PDF viewer overlay
constraints: Must work within a browser environment that supports PDF rendering.
Page navigation methods depend on the PDF viewer (e.g., built-in browser PDF viewer).
Large or complex PDFs may cause longer load times or require explicit waiting.
facts: The target PDF is from docs.house.gov, a U.S. House of Representatives document.
The goal is to read and retrieve the content on page 3 of the PDF.
procedures: Navigate to the specified PDF URL.
Wait for the PDF to fully load and render in the browser.
Go to the desired page (e.g., page 3) of the PDF document.
Extract the text content from the currently displayed page.
context_pack.summary: Extract text content from a specific page of a PDF document using a browser-based navigation and interaction workflow.

selected_candidate.name: extract_pdf_page_content

text.description: Extracted text from the specified page

postconditions: The text content of the specified page has been extracted and returned

selected_candidate.prompt_template: Navigate to the given PDF URL using a browser. Wait for the PDF to fully load. Then navigate to the specified page number (e.g., by simulating clicks on page controls or using the browser's PDF viewer API). Finally, extract all visible text from the current page view and return it as a string. If any step fails, return an error message describing the issue.

ctx2skill_evidence.selected_reason: The `extract_pdf_page_content` skill is selected because it directly addresses the core requirement of extracting content from a single specific page (page 3 in the original task). It is atomic, has clear input/output schemas, and can be easily composed into more complex skills like `extract_pdf_range_content`. Its prompt template and pre/post conditions are well-defined for reuse across different PDF documents. The score of 0.8 reflects its high relevance and reliability for the given trajectory.

# Browser-use normalized trajectory: extract-pdf-content

Source file: `examples/use-cases/extract_pdf_content.py`

## Original task excerpt

Objective: Navigate to the following UR, what is on page 3?

        URL: https://docs.house.gov/meetings/GO/GO00/20220929/115171/HHRG-117-GO00-20220929-SD010.pdf

## Normalized trajectory

1. Open the required browser context.
2. Observe page state and task constraints.
3. Choose the next safe browser action.
4. Execute search/click/type/select/upload/ext
